Tests the `Object.prototype.toString` method for `undefined` values.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of the `Object.prototype.toString` method when called with `undefined` as its argument. The test verifies that the method returns the string `"[object Undefined]"`, which is the expected output for the `undefined` type. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of JavaScript's type system and object prototype behavior. It is likely used to ensure that the JavaScript environment correctly implements this part of the ECMAScript specification.
